subject: Re: autoalign4warp issue in Matlab
body:
Hi Alister,

thanks a lot for the reply. Now I understand the issue and can try to work 
around it! 

best,
Alex

alist...@gmail.com schrieb am Dienstag, 29. März 2022 um 16:01:20 UTC+2:

> Hi Alex,
>
> It looks like you don’t have the image processing toolbox available on the 
> worker machines on a cluster that MATLAB is using - I would try to run the 
> program on a single machine if possible.
>
> I’m personally moving away from depending on MATLAB in my own workflows to 
> avoid these sorts of issues - it’s likely that making this toolbox 
> available on your configured cluster would cost you (your institution) a 
> significant amount of money.
>
> Sorry about that,
>
> Alister
>
> On 29 Mar 2022, at 13:23, 'Alex Hempelmann' via Warp <
> war...@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>
> Dear Warp users, 
> I have encountered an issue when using autoalign4warp in matlab, using the 
> HIV
> tutorial data set. 
> The command is executed and starts but will fail after while at
> ''Analyzing and transferring files to worker ..." See error log attached.
>
> According to the warning output I tried to attach the file to the pool, 
> prior to executing with
> >> addAttachedFiles(gcp, ['/opt/autoalign_dynamo/dautoalign4warp.m'])
> but this did not help at all, unfortunately.
>
>
> At this point I am not quite sure wether the issue lies within my dynamo 
> installation or parallel pool settings from matlab, as the error log also 
> states that files are missing in the generated alignments folder. 
> Does someone have suggestions what I could try next on this?
> Thanks a lot in advance!
> best,
> Alex
>
>
>
>
>
> <error matlab.png> 
>
